seoTitle: Bergalia vs Berkeley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Bergalia and Berkeley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bergalia and Berkeley.
heading: Bergalia vs Berkeley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bergalia, New South Wales and Berkeley, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bergalia has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Berkeley (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bergalia or Berkeley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bergalia has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Berkeley scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bergalia scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Berkeley scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bergalia or Berkeley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bergalia has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
